title: Urgent treatment centres and walk-in centres
content: Urgent treatment centres (UTCs) and walk-in centres handle urgent but non-life-threatening injuries and illnesses without an appointment: sprains and strains, suspected broken bones, minor burns, minor head injuries, cuts needing stitches, infections, and minor eye problems. They reduce A&E load. Find the nearest via NHS 111 or nhs.uk. They do not replace your GP for ongoing care.
